frontier: Erdős q(6): minimum number of edges in a 6-uniform pairwise-intersecting hypergraph with cover number exactly 6. The bracket is 14 ≤ q(6) ≤ 18.
action: Built and validated the exact witness verifier (q6_verifier.py): pairwise-intersection check O(m²·6) + exact 5-cover rejection via C(N,5) enumeration. Ran 6 fixtures (all pass): star τ=1, τ=2, disjoint edges, 3-cover, two 5-cover variants. Constructed PG(2,q) generator (pg2_verify.py) and proved τ(PG(2,q)) = q+1 for q=2,3,5. PG(2,5) verified as valid 31-edge 6-uniform intersecting hypergraph with τ=6 in 114ms.
verified:
6/6 fixture tests pass: verifier correctly rejects all known-bad non-witnesses
- PG(2,5): 31 edges, τ=6, pairwise-intersecting — confirmed valid witness (m=31, above bracket)
- PG(2,2): τ=3, PG(2,3): τ=4 — general theorem τ(PG(2,q)) = q+1 confirmed at all three tested prime orders
- No frontier call was needed or made (frontier_call_allowed=false)
result: Verifier is correct and fast. PG(2,5) provides a valid but suboptimal witness (m=31 vs. 14-18 bracket). The SAT/DRAT route was previously falsified (global τ constraint caused solver blowup). The productive path is orderly generation with canonical augmentation + τ-deficiency pruning, targeting m=14..17 with V≤29 (Barát Lemma 6.4). No improvement to the bracket was achieved in this scout pass.
next_gate: Implement or import an isomorph-free orderly-generation engine for 6-uniform intersecting families. For each generated candidate with 14 ≤ m ≤ 17, run the exact witness verifier. A single valid witness with m < 18 improves the bound; m=14 settles q(6)=14.
boundary_held: No production Atlas writes, external submissions, git pushes, or training. Research artifacts scoped to `research_sessions/res_20260715_052504_erdos-21-q6/`.
